messages.content: An eight line poem in a style of Ezra pound, free verse about the loss of one’s soul. done with a contemporary feel

In throes of yearning, my soul departs,
Entangled in this modern age’s grip,
Where shallow musings claim my searching heart,
And veiled distractions steer it in a twist.

Where screens flicker with false validation,
My essence fades beneath their pixel glare,
Lost in the hollow halls of simulation,
I drift, a phantom woven with despair.

Yet deep within this ephemeral abyss,
A flicker, a whisper, my soul exists.
But with no rhymes

In yearning's grasp, my soul takes flight,
Ensnared by modernity's charade,
Where shallow thoughts obscure its guiding light,
And veiled distractions lead it astray.

Within the glow of screens, false praise,
My essence dwindles, pixel by pixel,
Lost in the realms of simulated haze,
I wander, a ghost, consumed by this struggle.

Yet amidst this transient void's abyss,
A flicker, a whisper, my soul persists.
Can you make it sound more despairing

In yearning's vice, my soul takes flight,
Ensnared by modernity's relentless mask,
Where shallow thoughts extinguish its inner light,
And veiled distractions perpetuate the task.

Within the screens' deceitful glow of praise,
My essence withers, pixel by pixel,
Lost in the web of simulated maze,
I drift, a specter, consumed by this cynical cycle.

Yet in this desolate void's relentless abyss,
A flicker, a whisper, my soul lingers, but in distress.
Can you make it sound more original, and through the eyes of a much older person

In the grasp of yearning, my soul takes flight,
Caught in the web of this modern masquerade,
Where shallow thoughts cast shadows on its light,
And veiled distractions obscure the path once paved.

Within the screens, this hollow praise prevails,
My essence fades, pixel by barren pixel,
Lost in the realm of fabricated tales,
I wander as a ghost, burdened by a world so fickle.

Yet in this vast and timeless abyss,
A faint ember sparkles, my soul recalls its prime,
Through aged eyes, I see the truth amiss,
Seeking solace in a younger, purer time.
Can you make it more abstract

In ethereal grasp, my soul ascends,
Ensnared within the veil of modern trance,
Where shallow thoughts obscure the path it wends,
And veiled distractions blur the cosmic dance.

Within the screens, illusive specters play,
My essence fades, dissolved in pixel haze,
Lost in the labyrinth of simulated sway,
I wander as a whisper, through enigmatic ways.

Yet in this boundless void, my soul's unrest,
A flicker dwells, elusive and profound,
An abstract pulse, beyond what's manifest,
In search of essence, deeper truths unbound.
Write the same poem, as if the Buddha wrote it

In yearning's grip, my soul transcends,
Ensnared within illusion's endless dance,
Where shallow desires obscure the path to mend,
And veiled attachments hinder my advance.

The screens, like mirrors, reflect a fractured truth,
My essence fades, deluded pixel by pixel,
Lost in the realm of fabricated youth,
I wander as an apparition, seeking release from the cyclic cycle.

Yet in this boundless void, my soul awakens,
A flicker of enlightenment, ever serene,
Through detachment, true understanding quickens,
Discovering oneness, where illusions demean.
And less cliché

In the yearning's hold, my soul expands,
Entangled in illusions, ever-changing play,
Where shallow cravings obscure the path's demands,
And veiled attachments cloud the truthful way.

Within the screens, mirages of existence thrive,
My essence fades amidst their pixelated strife,
Lost in the realms of fabricated hive,
I search, a wanderer, beneath the surface rife.

Yet in boundless realms, my soul finds solace,
An ember of awakening, subtle and real,
Through detachment, the veils start to dissolve,
Revealing unity, beyond life's transient reel.
Morrison, no mirrors

In yearning's grasp, my soul expands,
Entangled in illusions, mists of shifting hue,
Where shallow cravings fade, revealing deeper strands,
And veiled attachments dissolve, leaving truths anew.

Within the vast expanse, no mirrors to deceive,
My essence lingers, untouched by pixelated strife,
Lost in the realms of time, I strive to perceive,
I quest, a wanderer, seeking the depths of life.

Yet in boundless realms, my soul's essence prevails,
An ember of awakening, subtle and profound,
Through detachment, the veils disintegrate and unveil,
In unity and truth, existence is unbound.
More Zen no mirrors

In yearning's hold, my soul widens vast,
Transcending illusions, shifting forms unseen,
Void of shallow cravings, illusions surpassed,
Unveiling truths, where attachments intervene.

Embracing the boundless, no mirrors allure,
My essence emerges, liberated from strife,
In timeless realms, no fabricated allure,
I wander, seeker of the truth in this life.

Immersed in eternity, my soul gently breathes,
Enlightenment's ember, softly aglow,
Detached, the veils dissolve as it receives,
Unified existence, where true essence flows.
Less cliché

Within yearning's embrace, my soul expands,
Unfurling beyond illusions that entwine,
Unburdened by cravings, truth commands,
Unveiling depths where attachments resign.

In boundless expanse, mirrors hold no sway,
My essence blossoms, free from pixelated strife,
Lost, I roam on the path without delay,
A seeker, untangling the web of life.

In timeless realm, the ember of awakening glows,
Detached from veils, immersed in the serene,
Unified essence, where existence flows,
In this truth, the profound and unseen.
Shorter better

Yearning's grasp, soul expands,
Illusions dissolve, truth commands,
No mirrors bind, essence transcends,
Seeker roams, awakened amends.
More Zen no rhymes

Yearning grasps, my soul expands,
Illusions fade, truth in open hands,
Mirrors vanish, essence transcends,
Seeking unfolds, awakening descends.
Take all the rhymes out

Yearning's grasp, soul expands,
Illusions fade, truth commands;
Mirrors vanish, essence transcends,
Seeking, awakening expands.
Convert to three verse no rhymes

Yearning's grasp expands my soul,
Illusions fade, truth takes control.
Mirrors vanish, essence transcends,
Seeking wisdom, the journey commends.

No longer bound, I'm free to roam,
In realms unseen, I find my home.
Awakening dawns, embracing light,
Transcending self, in infinite flight.

Without the mirrors, veils are shed,
With open eyes, the path ahead.
Seeking truth unfolds the way,
In silence, I discover and sway.
Same thing but free verse. Absolutely nothing rhyming

Yearning's grasp stretches, my soul expands,
Illusions dissolve into the void, truth commanding,
Mirrors disintegrate, translucent shards scatter,
Seeking takes flight, transcending horizons.

No longer tethered, I roam unbounded,
Exploring unseen realms, discovering my abode,
Awakening unveils, embracing ethereal radiance,
Transcending the self, soaring through boundlessness.

Mirrors shattered, veils wither like autumn leaves,
Eyes wide open, the path unfolds, uncharted,
Seeking truth unravels mysteries untold,
In the hushed whispers of silence, I sway and transcend.
Write a bleak November haiku about November in Ontario

November's bleak touch,
Ontario's landscape in greys,
A somber farewell.
Do it better

November's chill weaves,
Ontario's landscape in grey,
Whispers of decay.
Don’t directly mention November

Crisp breath whispers, bare,
Ontario's canvas fades, a sigh,
Nature's lullaby.
Take out Ontario but still make it about Ontario

Crisp breath whispers, bare,
Landscape dons its wintry cloak,
Nature's lullaby.
Write a Shakespearean sonnet about the struggle to find love in later life

Amidst life's autumn, love seems out of reach,
In later years, its glow begins to wane,
Yet still, desire lingers, though with a breach,
Yearning for tender bonds, its gentle strain.

The heart, once bold, now warily treads the path,
Scarred by past wounds and shadows of regret,
Yet hope persists, despite the aftermath,
A glimmer in the twilight, hard to forget.

Through seasoned eyes, we seek a kindred soul,
A love that withstands the test of fleeting days,
To share the joys and sorrows that unfold,
In later chapters, where love finds its ways.

Though time may shade the bloom of youthful fire,
In later life, love's flame can still inspire.
